test.command: uv run pytest -q && C:\Users\oimir\.cargo\bin\cargo.exe test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --release

lint.command: uv run ruff check . && C:\Users\oimir\.cargo\bin\cargo.exe clippy --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -- -D warnings

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document Windows-specific setup fallback. If .factory/init.sh fails or produces syntax errors on Windows, and the environment is already healthy (e.g. tests pass), workers can bypass the init script to avoid heavyweight reinstall.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker noted init.sh produced syntax error on Windows. Relied on healthy environment (uv run pytest -q passed).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update skill expectations to acknowledge that workers may inherit dirty worktrees from prior agents/runs, rather than always starting from a clean slate with TDD.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker in rewrite-benchmark-gate received a dirty worktree with the targeted hot-path change. Validating and refining was more practical than reverting to reconstruct a fresh failing test.
